codesign: errSecInternalComponent / security show-keychain-info: User interaction is not allowed
bugs.root_cause: This coding session's sandboxed shell cannot reach the Security Agent/securityd for any interactive keychain authorization, even after granting the codesign/security key partition-list ACL (Always Allow) on login.keychain-db. Not fixable via keychain config - it is a sandbox-level IPC restriction specific to this execution environment.
bugs.fix: No CLI fix available in this sandbox. Working alternative: trigger Xcode's own GUI process to build via `osascript -e 'tell application "Xcode" to activate' -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "r" using command down'` (requires Accessibility permission granted once in System Settings). Xcode GUI process is not subject to this sandbox restriction and can codesign normally.

bugs.error_message: User-reported: a created team is not listed in TeamsListView
bugs.root_cause: The custom ModelContainer built in BlindensportGrazApp.init() was never attached to the WindowGroup via .modelContainer(modelContainer) - present in the original pre-existing code, not introduced this session. Without that attachment, @Query/@Environment(\\.modelContext) usage across different views can resolve inconsistent/ephemeral contexts instead of one shared container, so data inserted in one view is not reliably visible from another.
bugs.fix: Added .modelContainer(modelContainer) to the WindowGroup scene in BlindensportGrazApp.swift.

bugs.error_message: 'main' attribute cannot be used in a module that contains top-level code
bugs.root_cause: SwiftPM treats a source file literally named `main.swift` in an executable target as implicit top-level-code script, which conflicts with a separate `@main`-annotated type in the same target — not a general Swift language restriction, just SwiftPM's naming special-case.
bugs.fix: Renamed the file from main.swift to RootCLI.swift (keeping the @main struct); build succeeded immediately after.

bugs.error_message: User-reported: 'I can not assign a member of Grazer VSC to a newly created team. The members are not listed.'
bugs.root_cause: TeamMembership.user required a registered User (not the ClubMember roster), and AddMemberView's picker only ever queried [User]. Most Grazer VSC roster entries (ClubMember) have no matching app account, since they're admin-entered roster data, not sign-ups — so they never appeared as assignable team members. Not a code defect so much as a design gap: two separate 'member' concepts (registered User vs. roster ClubMember) with no bridge between them for team assignment.
bugs.fix: Made TeamMembership.user optional and added an optional TeamMembership.clubMember, exactly one set per membership. Added TeamMembership.displayName/.subtitle computed helpers so rendering code doesn't need to know which side is set. AddMemberView now shows two Picker sections: 'Registrierte Benutzer' and 'Grazer VSC Mitglieder ohne Konto'. CloudKitSync publishes/resolves either userID or clubMemberID on the TeamMembership record. EventParticipation was deliberately left untouched (self-registering for an event still requires an account).

bugs.error_message: User-reported: 'The membership assignment to a training is not working.'
bugs.root_cause: myTeams (in AddEventView/AddTrainingView/AddTournamentView) and visibleEvents/visibleTrainings/visibleTournaments (in the corresponding ListViews) filtered teams/items down to only ones the current user personally has a TeamMembership for. AddTeamView never creates a TeamMembership for the team's creator, so an admin who creates a new team can't assign it to an event/training/tournament (the Team picker section doesn't render at all when myTeams is empty) and can't see items already scoped to that team either. Same bug independently present in all three files; not training-specific, just reported there first. Same root cause class as the ClubMember team-assignment bug fixed earlier the same day (bug-045).
bugs.fix: myTeams and every visible* computed property now bypass the personal-membership filter when currentUser.role == "admin" (matches CLAUDE.md's documented 'admin - Full access to all features'); coach keeps the original team-restricted behavior, which CLAUDE.md documents as intentional.

bugs.error_message: User-reported: 'The app says: no team assigned, when i want to add a member to a training.'
bugs.root_cause: TrainingDetailView/EventDetailView/TournamentDetailView had no UI to assign or change a team after creation — only the Add*View creation forms had a team picker, and that picker was itself broken until bug-054 was fixed. So a training created before that fix (or with 'Fur alle sichtbar' chosen) was permanently team-less; the admin-only member list correctly reported 'no team assigned' since MemberListView derives members entirely from the item's team(s). Separately discovered while fixing this: editing any field on an existing Event/Training/Tournament never pushed the change to CloudKit at all — pushEvent/pushTraining/pushTournament were only ever called once, at creation, in the Add*View.
bugs.fix: Added a team-assignment Picker to TrainingDetailView (single team, state-backed via selectedTeamID: UUID? to match the existing UUID-tag convention) and a multi-team toggle list to EventDetailView/TournamentDetailView (same 'Beteiligte Teams' pattern already used in the Add*View forms). Added .onDisappear { modelContext.save(); CloudKitSync.shared.push...(...) } to all three detail views so any edit, not just team reassignment, now syncs to CloudKit on leaving the screen.

bugs.error_message: User-reported: 'the export of members of a training is not possible. The app crashes.'
bugs.root_cause: ActivityView (UIViewControllerRepresentable wrapping UIActivityViewController, used by MemberListView.exportTeilnehmerliste to share the generated .xlsx via a second .sheet) never configured popoverPresentationController.sourceView/sourceRect. UIActivityViewController defaults to popover presentation style on iPad (this app supports iPad via TARGETED_DEVICE_FAMILY '1,2' in project.yml) — with no popover anchor, UIKit throws an uncaught NSInvalidArgumentException at presentation time and the app hard-crashes. This is a UIKit-level fatal exception, not a thrown Swift Error, so MemberListView.exportTeilnehmerliste's existing do/catch (bug-066) could never have caught it — the export/zip-generation logic itself was already verified correct (see cerebrum 2026-07-17 TeilnehmerInnenliste entry).
bugs.fix: ActivityView.makeUIViewController now sets popover.sourceView (to the key window's root view) and popover.sourceRect (centered) whenever controller.popoverPresentationController is non-nil, with permittedArrowDirections = [] since there's no specific anchor button to point at. Verified: clean xcodebuild for iphonesimulator (BUILD SUCCEEDED, 0 errors), then installed + launched on an iPad Air (M4) simulator and confirmed the process stays alive (no immediate crash). Could not drive the full export flow end-to-end in-session since the app requires real Apple ID / CloudKit sign-in, not automatable from a coding sandbox.

bugs.error_message: User-reported (follow-up to bug-070): 'the app crashed again' on the export flow, clarified as a permanent freeze (not a crash) on iPhone von Franz with VoiceOver running
bugs.root_cause: MemberListView presented its own share sheet (ActivityView/UIActivityViewController) as a second .sheet nested directly on top of the first .sheet (MemberListView itself, presented from Training/TournamentDetailView). This sheet-on-a-sheet pattern froze the main thread only when VoiceOver was running (VoiceOvers synchronous accessibility-tree recompute does not tolerate a second modal presented while the first is still resolving) -- never reproduced with VoiceOver off, and the bug-070 iPad-popover fix was unrelated/insufficient since this device is an iPhone. No crash/hang report was ever generated on-device (confirmed via repeated xcrun devicectl device info files --domain-type systemCrashLogs polling and a manually-triggered sysdiagnose) because iOS does not watchdog-kill an app that is merely stuck in the foreground, only for lifecycle-transition timeouts.
bugs.fix: MemberListView no longer manages its own share-sheet state -- it now takes an onExported: (URL) -> Void closure and calls dismiss() + onExported(url) on successful export instead of presenting ActivityView itself. TrainingDetailView and TournamentDetailView each added exportedFileURL/showShareSheet state and now present the share sheet from .sheet(isPresented: showMemberList binding, onDismiss: { if exportedFileURL != nil { showShareSheet = true } }) -- i.e. only after the member-list sheet has fully finished dismissing, eliminating the nested-sheet presentation entirely. Verified: clean xcodebuild for iphonesimulator (BUILD SUCCEEDED), installed + launched on iPhone 17 simulator, process stays alive. Could not verify the VoiceOver-specific freeze is gone end-to-end in-session (requires real device + VoiceOver + full login/team/training/attendance setup, not automatable from this sandbox) -- user should retest the export flow with VoiceOver on before considering this closed.

bugs.error_message: User-reported (second follow-up): export still freezes after the nested-sheet fix, clarified as freezing immediately on tapping the export button itself, before the member-list sheet ever closes
bugs.root_cause: UNKNOWN (not yet found). Ruled out two hypotheses via direct testing: (1) a suspected ZIPFoundation 0.9.20 infinite loop in Data.compress when a file size is an exact multiple of the 16384-byte write-chunk size -- disproven with a standalone SPM test harness exercising the real ZIPFoundation addEntry() across boundary sizes with a semaphore timeout, all completed instantly; (2) that TeilnehmerlisteExporter.export() itself hangs on some realistic input -- disproven with a new BlindensportGrazTests unit test target calling the real in-app export function against realistic German/Austrian names (umlauts, ss, hyphens) across 0-30 attendees, all completed in single-digit milliseconds. The freeze is very likely a live-UI/VoiceOver-runtime issue that cannot be reproduced headlessly -- next step is Simulator reproduction with VoiceOver actually enabled, not further static reasoning about the export function.
bugs.fix: No fix yet -- root cause still open. Kept the earlier compressionMethod .deflate -> .none change (reasonable defensive simplification, not proven necessary). Added permanent regression-test infrastructure: BlindensportGrazTests target (project.yml) + TeilnehmerlisteExportTests.swift, runnable via xcodebuild test -scheme BlindensportGrazTests -sdk iphonesimulator -- keep this test updated as a first check for any future export-related bug report before assuming the export logic itself is at fault.
Rewrote 13→6 lines (8 removed) | Also: Button {; exportTeilnehmerliste(exportContext)

bugs.error_message: User confirmed: plain text ShareLink works fine under VoiceOver on this same screen; only the custom Excel-export UIActivityViewController path freezes
bugs.root_cause: Isolated to UIActivityViewController presentation (via a hand-rolled ActivityView UIViewControllerRepresentable, shown through .sheet) specifically under VoiceOver -- proven NOT to be TeilnehmerlisteExporter.export() itself (unit-tested, always fast) and NOT explained by the ZIPFoundation compression theory (disproven, see bug-073). SwiftUI native ShareLink, used elsewhere in the exact same view for plain text, works reliably under this users VoiceOver setup, while the custom UIActivityViewController wrapper for a file URL does not -- most likely VoiceOver-driven document preview/QuickLook generation for a real file getting stuck, exact UIKit-internal mechanism not further isolated since a clean fix was available.
bugs.fix: Replaced the entire custom ActivityView/onExported-callback/parent-onDismiss apparatus with SwiftUI native ShareLink(item: url), the exact mechanism already proven to work for this user. MemberListView now eagerly generates the export file into @State via .task(id:) as soon as exportContext is available (proven near-instant) and shows ShareLink(item: exportedFileURL) once ready, replacing the old tap-a-button-then-wait flow. Deleted the now-dead ActivityView struct and unused UIKit/SwiftUI imports from TeilnehmerlisteExport.swift. TrainingDetailView/TournamentDetailView reverted to a single plain .sheet(isPresented: ) with no onDismiss/exportedFileURL/showShareSheet coordination, since the parent no longer needs to present a second sheet at all. Verified: xcodebuild build succeeded, BlindensportGrazTests all pass, simulator install+launch stays alive. NOT YET re-confirmed by user on the physical device with VoiceOver -- awaiting their retest.
